If Venus appears with the lustre of fire, there will be danger
from fire; if he is red in colour, there will be war; if he is golden
in hue, diseases will erupt; if he looks green or tawny, people will
suffer greatly from asthma and cough; and if he appears grey like
ashes, coarse or dark in colour, not a drop of water will fall from
the sky.
[The metre is qtMh]
qfef5?5RTia^g: i

Course of Venus IX 113
Should Venus be possessed of the lustre of curds, Kumuda
(a kind of lotus) flower or the Moon, bright and expanding rays,
a full and large orb and good motion i.e. not retrograde, and
situated to the north of a star or planet, and be free from disturb-
ances like portents, and be victorious in a planetary fight, he would
